revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社| 名称=神社| 所在地=岐阜県高山市丹生川町山口字月本145| 祭神=[[オオヤマツミ|大山津見神]] など| 社格=式内社 ...'
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=神社| 所在地=岐阜県高山市丹生川町山口字月本145| 祭神=[[オオヤマツミ|大山津見神]] など| 社格=式内社 郷社| 例祭=9月12日| 画像= }}
'''槻本神社'''（つきもとじんじゃ）は[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]（旧・[[大野郡 (岐阜県)|大野郡]][[丹生川村]]）にある[[神社]]である。
[[飛騨国]][[大野郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]、槻本神社である。
御神木の[[スギ|杉]]は岐阜県天然記念物に指定されている。樹齢1,200年以上と推測され、この木に危害を与えた者はすぐに病死するという言い伝えがある。
== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。言い伝えでは[[ケヤキ|槻]]の大木がご神体であったという。後に社殿が造られた後も存在していたという（槻の大木では無く、杉の大木の言い伝えもある）。
現在の社殿は[[元文]]5年（[[1740年]]）の改築という。[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に郷社となる。
== 祭神 == *[[オオヤマツミ|大山津見神]] *[[スサノオ|櫛御気野神]] *[[建御名方神|建御名方刀美神]] *[[八坂刀売神]]
別の説としては、[[保食神]]、月元大明神がある。
== 交通 == *[[高山本線]][[高山駅]]より北東へ約7km。 **[[国道158号]]、丹生川町山口から山口谷川（[[神通川|宮川]]支流）沿いを東進。 **国道158号、丹生川町坊方より南下。飛騨農園街道の丹生川トンネルをくぐり西へ約1km。
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきもとししんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:高山市]]

{{神社| 名称=神社| 所在地=岐阜県高山市丹生川町山口字月本145| 祭神=[[オオヤマツミ|大山津見神]] など| 社格=式内社 郷社| 例祭=9月12日| 画像= }}
'''槻本神社'''（つきもとじんじゃ）は[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]（旧・[[大野郡 (岐阜県)|大野郡]][[丹生川村]]）にある[[神社]]である。
[[飛騨国]][[大野郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]、槻本神社である。
御神木の[[スギ|杉]]は岐阜県天然記念物に指定されている。樹齢1,200年以上と推測され、この木に危害を与えた者はすぐに病死するという言い伝えがある。
== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。言い伝えでは[[ケヤキ|槻]]の大木がご神体であったという。後に社殿が造られた後も存在していたという（槻の大木では無く、杉の大木の言い伝えもある）。
現在の社殿は[[元文]]5年（[[1740年]]）の改築という。[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に郷社となる。
== 祭神 == *[[オオヤマツミ|大山津見神]] *[[スサノオ|櫛御気野神]] *[[建御名方神|建御名方刀美神]] *[[八坂刀売神]]
別の説としては、[[保食神]]、月元大明神がある。
== 交通 == *[[高山本線]][[高山駅]]より北東へ約7km。 **[[国道158号]]、丹生川町山口から山口谷川（[[神通川|宮川]]支流）沿いを東進。 **国道158号、丹生川町坊方より南下。飛騨農園街道の丹生川トンネルをくぐり西へ約1km。
{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきもとししんしや}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:郷社]] [[Category:高山市]]

{{神社| 名称=神社| 所在地=岐阜県高山市丹生川町山口字月本145| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=[[オオヤマツミ|大山津見神]] など| 社格=式内社 郷社| 例祭=9月12日| 画像= }}
'''槻本神社'''（つきもとじんじゃ）は[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]（旧・[[大野郡 (岐阜県)|大野郡]][[丹生川村]]）にある[[神社]]である。
[[飛騨国]][[大野郡 (岐阜県)|大野郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]、槻本神社である。
御神木の[[スギ|杉]]は岐阜県天然記念物に指定されている<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=槻本神社のスギ |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>。樹齢1,200年以上と推測され、この木に危害を与えた者はすぐに病死するという言い伝えがある。
== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。言い伝えでは[[ケヤキ|槻]]の大木がご神体であったという。後に社殿が造られた後も存在していたという（槻の大木では無く、杉の大木の言い伝えもある）。
現在の社殿は[[元文]]5年（[[1740年]]）の改築という。[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に郷社となる。
== 祭神 == *[[オオヤマツミ|大山津見神]] *[[スサノオ|櫛御気野神]] *[[建御名方神|建御名方刀美神]] *[[八坂刀売神]]
別の説としては、[[保食神]]、月元大明神がある。
== 文化財 == *槻本神社のスギ（県指定天然記念物）<ref name="sugi"/> *槻本神社々号碑（市指定史跡）<ref>{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=史跡・名勝 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== 交通 == *[[高山本線]][[高山駅]]より北東へ約7km。 **[[国道158号]]、丹生川町山口から山口谷川（[[神通川|宮川]]支流）沿いを東進。 **国道158号、丹生川町坊方より南下。飛騨農園街道の丹生川トンネルをくぐり西へ約1km。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}

{{神社| 名称=槻本神社| 画像={{画像募集中|cat=高山市}}| 所在地=岐阜県高山市丹生川町山口字月本145| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=[[オオヤマツミ|大山津見神]] など| 社格=式内社 郷社| 例祭=9月12日| }}
'''槻本神社'''（つきもとじんじゃ）は[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]にある[[神社]]である。
[[飛騨国]][[大野郡]]の[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]、槻本神社である。
御神木の[[スギ|杉]]は岐阜県天然記念物に指定されている<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=槻本神社のスギ |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref>。樹齢1,200年以上と推測され、この木に危害を与えた者はすぐに病死するという言い伝えがある。
== 沿革 == 創建時期は不明。言い伝えでは[[ケヤキ|槻]]の大木がご神体であったという。後に社殿が造られた後も存在していたという（槻の大木では無く、杉の大木の言い伝えもある）。
現在の社殿は[[元文]]5年（[[1740年]]）の改築という。[[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に郷社となる。
== 祭神 == *[[オオヤマツミ|大山津見神]] *[[スサノオ|櫛御気野神]] *[[建御名方神|建御名方刀美神]] *[[八坂刀売神]]

== 交通 == *[[高山本線]][[高山駅]]より北東へ約7km。 **[[国道158号]]、丹生川町山口から山口谷川（[[神通川|宮川]]支流）沿いを東進。 **国道158号、丹生川町坊方より南下。飛騨農園街道の丹生川トンネルをくぐり西へ約1km。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[飛騨国の式内社一覧]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name=Tsukimoto Shrine | Image=[[File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg|300px]] | Location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | Coordinates={{Coord|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}} | Deities=[[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] and others | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Festival=September 12 }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]'s {{ill|Ōno District (Gifu)|ja|大野郡}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's Name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

{{Shrine | Name=Tsukimoto Shrine | Image=[[File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg|300px]] | Location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | Coordinates={{Coord|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}} | Deities=[[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] and others | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Festival=September 12 }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]'s {{ill|Ōno District (Gifu)|ja|大野郡}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's Name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.

{{Shrine | Name=Tsukimoto Shrine | image=[[File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | Coordinates={{Coord|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}} | Deities=[[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] and others | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Festival=September 12 }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]'s {{ill|Ōno District (Gifu)|ja|大野郡}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's Name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name=Tsukimoto Shrine | image=[[File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | Coordinates={{Coord|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}} | Deities=[[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] and others | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Festival=September 12 }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]'s {{ill|Ōno District (Gifu)|ja|大野郡}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's Name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's Name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.

== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name=Tsukimoto Shrine | image=[[File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | Coordinates={{Coord|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}} | Deities=[[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] and others | Shrine Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Festival=September 12 }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]'s {{ill|Ōno District (Gifu)|ja|大野郡}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's Name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}} | Deities=[[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] and others | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Festival=September 12 }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]'s {{ill|Ōno District (Gifu)|ja|大野郡}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.

The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}} | deity=[[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] and others | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Festival=September 12 }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]'s {{ill|Ōno District (Gifu)|ja|大野郡}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}} | deity=[[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] and others | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Festival=September 12 }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]'s {{ill|Ōno District (Gifu)|ja|大野郡}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}} | deity=[[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] and others | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Festival=September 12 }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]'s {{ill|Ōno District (Gifu)|ja|大野郡}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.

== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}} | deity=[[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] and others | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Festival=September 12 }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]'s {{ill|Ōno District (Gifu)|ja|大野郡}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}} | deity=[[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] and others | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Festival=September 12 }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]'s {{ill|Ōno District (Gifu)|ja|大野郡}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}} | deity=[[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] and others | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Festival=September 12 }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]'s {{ill|Ōno District (Gifu)|ja|大野郡}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}} | deity=[[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] and others | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Festival=September 12 }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]'s {{ill|Ōno District (Gifu)|ja|大野郡}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>

Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}} | deity=[[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] and others | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Festival=September 12 }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]'s {{ill|Ōno District (Gifu)|ja|大野郡}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}} | deity=[[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] and others | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Festival=September 12 }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]'s {{ill|Ōno District (Gifu)|ja|大野郡}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}} | deity=[[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] and others | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Festival=September 12 }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]'s {{ill|Ōno District (Gifu)|ja|大野郡}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}} | deity=[[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] and others | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Festival=September 12 }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]'s {{ill|Ōno District (Gifu)|ja|大野郡}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg|300px]] | location=[[Gifu Prefecture]] [[Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36|9|39.87|N|137|19|7.26|E|scale:30000}} | deity=[[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] and others | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} | Main Festival=September 12 }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama City]], [[Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} for [[Hida Province]]'s {{ill|Ōno District (Gifu)|ja|大野郡}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|ja|スギ}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun era|ja|元文}} (1740). In the 4th year of the [[Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[Takayama Station]] on the [[Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:槻本神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11542024]] Wikidata name: Tsukimoto-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg | location=[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36.161083|N|137.318639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Main Festival=September 12 | prefecture={{ill|Takayama|qid=Q622594}} }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
{{Shinto-stub}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.

Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
