<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:槻本神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11542024]] Wikidata name: Tsukimoto-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg | location=[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36.161083|N|137.318639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Main Festival=September 12 | prefecture={{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
{{Shinto-stub}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.

In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:槻本神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11542024]] Wikidata name: Tsukimoto-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg | location=[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36.161083|N|137.318639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Main Festival=September 12 | prefecture={{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}

The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.

Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

== Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:槻本神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11542024]] Wikidata name: Tsukimoto-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg | location=[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36.161083|N|137.318639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Main Festival=September 12 | prefecture={{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. '''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.

The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.

== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.

<!--Tsukimoto Shrine--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11542024}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:槻本神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11542024]] Wikidata name: Tsukimoto-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg | location=[[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]] [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36.161083|N|137.318639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Main Festival=September 12 | prefecture={{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu Prefecture]]. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for [[:en:Hida Province|Hida Province]]'s [[:en:Ōno District, Gifu|Ōno District (Gifu)]].
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
{{Shinto-stub}}

The shrine's sacred cedar tree ([[:en:Cryptomeria|Cryptomeria]]) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]] tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] (1740). In the 4th year of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as [[:en:Ukemochi|Ukemochi no Kami]] and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of [[:en:Takayama Station|Takayama Station]] on the [[:en:Takayama Main Line|Takayama Main Line]]. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the [[:en:Jinzū River|Miyagawa]]) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
{{Shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|槻本神社|version=98148033|comment=Imported full ja history}}
