== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.

<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11542024]] Wikidata name: Tsukimoto-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg | location={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36.161083|N|137.318639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Main Festival=September 12 | prefecture={{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi no Kami}} and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.

<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11542024]] Wikidata name: Tsukimoto-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg | location={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36.161083|N|137.318639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Main Festival=September 12 | prefecture={{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi no Kami}} and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> <!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11542024]] Wikidata name: Tsukimoto-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsukimoto Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Tsukimoto shrine.jpg | location={{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}} [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi Aza Tsukimoto 145 | coordinates={{Coord|36.161083|N|137.318639|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | Main Festival=September 12 | prefecture={{ill|Takayama|WD=Q622594}} }}
'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi no Kami}} and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
{{Shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|槻本神社|version=98148033|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi no Kami}} and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi no Kami}} and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi no Kami}} and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
{{Shinto-stub}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi no Kami}} and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi no Kami}} and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi no Kami}} and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
{{Shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|槻本神社|version=98148033|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi no Kami}} and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
{{Shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|槻本神社|version=98148033|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi no Kami}} and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
{{Shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|槻本神社|version=98148033|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi no Kami}} and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
{{Shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|槻本神社|version=98148033|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi no Kami}} and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi no Kami}} and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
{{Shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|槻本神社|version=98148033|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi no Kami}} and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
{{Shinto-stub}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi no Kami}} and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi no Kami}} and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
{{Shinto-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|槻本神社|version=98148033|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Tsukimoto Shrine''' (槻本神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Takayama City|Takayama City]], {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is identified as the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} "Tsukimoto Shrine" in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} for {{ill|Hida Province|en|Hida Province|WD=Q1056535|lt=Hida Province|1=Hida Province}}'s {{ill|Ōno District, Gifu|en|Ōno District, Gifu|WD=Q31671|lt=Ōno District (Gifu)|1=Ōno District, Gifu}}.
The shrine's sacred cedar tree ({{ill|Cryptomeria|en|Cryptomeria|WD=Q147388|lt=Cryptomeria|1=Cryptomeria}}) is designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi">{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/takayamsi/tukimoto.html |title=Tsukimoto Shrine's Cedar Tree |publisher=Gifu Prefecture |accessdate=2013-04-20}}</ref> The tree is estimated to be over 1,200 years old, and local lore warns that those who harm it will quickly fall ill and die.
== History == The date of the shrine's founding is unknown. According to legend, the original sacred object was a giant {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova|1=Zelkova serrata}} tree (槻), which continued to exist even after the construction of the shrine buildings. Another version of the legend attributes the sacred status to a giant cedar tree instead of a zelkova.
The current shrine structure was rebuilt in the 5th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun|1=Genbun}} (1740). In the 4th year of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1871), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.
== Enshrined Deities == * [[Ōyama-tsumi|Ōyama-tsumi no Kami]] * [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Kushimikeno no Kami]] * [[Takeminakata|Takeminakata no Kami]] * [[Yasakatome|Yasakatome no Kami]]
Alternate theories identify the deities as {{ill|Ukemochi|en|Ukemochi|WD=Q2280323|lt=Ukemochi no Kami|1=Ukemochi}} and Tsukimoto Daimyōjin.
== Cultural Properties == * **Cedar Tree of Tsukimoto Shrine** - Designated as a Gifu Prefecture Natural Monument.<ref name="sugi" /> * **Monument Inscribed with the Shrine's name** - Designated as a Takayama City Historic Site.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/bunkazai/bunkazai/siseki/index.htm |title=Historic Sites and Scenic Spots |publisher=Takayama City |accessdate=2013-04-23}}</ref>
== Access == * Approximately 7 km northeast of {{ill|Takayama Station|en|Takayama Station|WD=Q874020|lt=Takayama Station|1=Takayama Station}} on the {{ill|Takayama Main Line|en|Takayama Main Line|WD=Q1188357|lt=Takayama Main Line|1=Takayama Main Line}}. ** Follow [[:en:National Route 158|National Route 158]] eastward along the Yamaguchi Valley (a tributary of the {{ill|Jinzū River|en|Jinzū River|WD=Q1156518|lt=Miyagawa|1=Jinzū River}}) from Nyūkawa Town, Yamaguchi. ** Alternatively, travel south from National Route 158 in Nyūkawa Town, Bōgata. Pass through the Nyūkawa Tunnel on the Hida Farm Road, then head west for about 1 km.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|ja|飛騨国の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Hida Province|qq=}}
{{Shinto-stub}}
